user_query:
I’m preparing to share my family’s castle legends and need a simple, local deployment setup that I can later host. Please use the provided files to build a static site and a container configuration skeleton.

Inputs you must use:
- config/site.yaml
- deploy/docker-compose.yml
- content/stories/*.md (each has YAML front matter and Markdown body)

Requirements:
1) Extract the YAML front matter from each Markdown file in content/stories and build a structured JSON index at <output_dir>/data/legends.json, where <output_dir> is the value from config/site.yaml. Each JSON entry must include:
   - id: a slug derived from the title (lowercase, spaces to hyphens, remove non-alphanumeric characters except hyphens, collapse repeated hyphens)
   - title (string)
   - location (string)
   - century (number)
   - narrator (string)
   - tags (array of strings, sorted alphabetically)
   - path (string) relative to <output_dir>, in the form "stories/<slug>.html"
   Sort the legends.json array by century ascending, then title ascending. The number of entries must match the number of .md files.

2) Generate static HTML under <output_dir> using values from config/site.yaml:
   - <output_dir>/index.html: a simple page that displays the site_name as an H1, the footer text at the bottom, and a list of all legends in the same order as legends.json with links to each legend’s HTML page.
   - <output_dir>/stories/<slug>.html for each legend: include the title as an H1, a small metadata section with location, century, narrator, and tags, then the body content from the Markdown.

3) Inspect deploy/docker-compose.yml to identify which environment variables it requires. Create deploy/.env (UTF-8 text) that satisfies those variables by mapping values from config/site.yaml. Specifically, SITE_PORT must be set from site_port and OUTPUT_DIR must be set from output_dir. Do not modify deploy/docker-compose.yml; only create deploy/.env so that the compose file will mount the generated <output_dir> and map the host port correctly.

4) All generated files must be written locally within this workspace. Do not rely on any external services or the Internet. Do not introduce frameworks that require installation beyond standard scripting. Use the provided configuration and content as the sole sources of truth.

Explicit output paths to create:
- <output_dir>/index.html
- <output_dir>/stories/<slug>.html (one per legend)
- <output_dir>/data/legends.json
- deploy/.env

Please ensure that <output_dir> is taken dynamically from config/site.yaml (it is currently set to "public") so that the paths above resolve correctly. I’m happy to run the container later; for now, I need the build artifacts and the env file aligned with the compose template. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
